steps.detail: Spawned manager agent (haiku/high) for tasks: a402f33a9ad9

task_snapshots.title: [DECOMPOSE] [RETRY 2] [RETRY 1] 333d-03: Agent exit logic for rebalancing
task_snapshots.description:
A large task needs to be decomposed into 3-5 smaller, atomic subtasks.

## Original large task (id=4badf2ea6dca)
**Title:** [RETRY 2] [RETRY 1] 333d-03: Agent exit logic for rebalancing
**Role:** backend
**Description:**
[retry:2] Implement agent exit triggers when a role has no more open tasks. Prevents idle agent accumulation.

- If role.tasks == 0 and agent.active, agent should exit gracefully
- Check every tick: if a role has more agents than tasks, do not spawn more
- Ensure clean shutdown without orphaning tasks

[subtask of 15d83a329576]

**Completion signal**: Agents exit when their role queue empties; test shows idle agents don't linger after task completion.

## Your job
1. Read the task description carefully
2. Identify 3-5 specific, atomic subtasks (each completable in one agent session, < 30 min)
3. Each subtask should target specific files and have clear completion criteria
4. Create each subtask via the task server:
```bash
curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8052/tasks -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"title": "...", "description": "... [subtask of 4badf2ea6dca]", "role": "backend", "priority": 0, "scope": "small", "complexity": "medium"}'
```
5. After creating all subtasks, exit.

IMPORTANT: Each subtask description MUST include '[subtask of 4badf2ea6dca]' so it can be tracked back to the original task.
